authorized_effects: Changing the two model default literals in ai_lab/providers/settings.py.
Updating the tests that asserted the previous defaults, and adding a test that both defaults appear in the retained catalog payloads.
Recording the reasoning in source comments beside each default so a future reader sees why the value was chosen without leaving the file.
blocked_effects: No routing, escalation, or per-role model selection: those remain Slice C and later.
No change to provider call semantics, parameters, or the max-tokens default.
No claim that the chosen models are better, cheaper, or more capable than the prior ones: no captured evidence supports any such comparison.
No inference that gpt-5.6-terra is a middle tier; that account is unverified and is recorded as such.
rationale: claude-sonnet-4-5 was absent from the captured Anthropic listing, which carries claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929 instead, while every AI-Lab call using the bare alias succeeded. The default was simultaneously stale (released 2025-09-29 per the provider's own created_at, ten months before this decision) and unlisted.
claude-sonnet-5 is present in the captured listing with created_at 2026-06-29, the most recent Sonnet-tier entry.

claude-opus-4-8 was deliberately NOT chosen although it is the most recent Opus-tier entry. The drafting executor in AI-Lab sessions reports itself as Claude Opus 4.8. Configuring the reviewer slot to that identity would collapse reviewer and author into one ModelIdentity, firing ABS-0004 C3 (no self-adjudication, executor-generic) on every review of drafted work, and the collapse would be invisible to every existing test. Preserving identity separation outranks tier recency for this slot.

gpt-5.6-terra is present in the captured OpenAI listing. Its tier relative to gpt-5.6-luna and gpt-5.6-sol is NOT established by any captured evidence: the OpenAI listing carries only id and owned_by, with no dates, display names, tiers, capabilities, or pricing.

The tier account used to choose terra comes from ADVISOR-0000, whose property claims are classified unverifiable and whose existence claims were confirmed only as names present in the listing. Acting on it here is disclosed rather than concealed: a model default is low consequence, immediately reversible, and self-testing, because a wrong name fails on first call.
Cost is unknown for both new defaults. No captured source publishes pricing, so no cost comparison against the prior defaults is possible from evidence AI-Lab holds.
required_next_governance: If cost matters to a future decision, a source that publishes pricing must be admitted first; the models listing does not carry it.
If a later slice gates on catalog presence (C2 catalog admission), it must not treat listing absence as invalidity: AI-Lab's own prior default was absent from the listing while working.
Any future move of the Claude slot to the drafting executor's own reported identity requires explicit adjudication of the C3 consequence recorded here.
selection_effect: Provider calls on all AI-Lab paths now default to claude-sonnet-5 and gpt-5.6-terra. Environment overrides AI_LAB_CLAUDE_MODEL and AI_LAB_OPENAI_MODEL continue to take precedence, and per-call overrides are unchanged.
summary: AI_LAB_CLAUDE_MODEL moves from claude-sonnet-4-5 to claude-sonnet-5 and AI_LAB_OPENAI_MODEL from gpt-5 to gpt-5.6-terra. Both prior values were literals chosen when ai_lab/providers/settings.py was written and never revisited, which is the deficiency GAP-0005 describes: model identity as a settings string with no ModelIdentity behind it. The first live catalog capture (2026-07-23) supplied the evidence for revisiting them. Both new values are present in the captured listings; a test now checks that fact against the retained payloads on every run.
title: Provider model defaults advanced on first live catalog evidence
